riscv.label: // risc-v
riscv.heading: RISC-V Emulation (ESP32-C3)
riscv.lead: ESP32-C3, XIAO ESP32-C3, and C3 SuperMini boards run on the RISC-V RV32IMC architecture at 160 MHz. Velxio emulates them through the same QEMU-based backend as the Xtensa ESP32 family, using libqemu-riscv32 with the esp32c3-picsimlab machine target. A separate TypeScript ISA implementation lives in the repo for unit-testing RV32IMC instruction decoding, but it is not the production emulation path.
riscv.isaRv32i: RV32I: Full base integer instruction set (ALU, load/store, branches, JAL/JALR)
riscv.isaRv32m: RV32M: Multiply/divide: MUL, MULH, MULHSU, MULHU, DIV, DIVU, REM, REMU
riscv.isaRv32c: RV32C: 16-bit compressed instructions: C.LI, C.ADDI, C.LUI, C.J, C.JAL, C.BEQZ, C.BNEZ, C.MV, C.ADD, C.JR, C.JALR, C.LW, C.SW, C.LWSP, C.SWSP, C.SLLI, C.ADDI4SPN
riscv.compileFlowLead: When you click Compile + Run for an ESP32-C3 board:
riscv.compileStep1: The backend compiles your sketch with arduino-cli using the esp32:esp32 core.
riscv.compileStep2: The resulting binary is a merged 4 MB flash image: bootloader at 0x1000, partition table at 0x8000, application at 0x10000.
riscv.compileStep3: The frontend's esp32ImageParser.ts finds the app at offset 0x10000, reads the 24-byte ESP32 image header (magic 0xE9), and extracts all segments (load address + data).
riscv.compileStep4: Each segment is written into the correct memory region (IROM, DROM, DRAM, or IRAM) of the Esp32C3Simulator.
riscv.compileStep5: The CPU starts executing from the entry point specified in the image header.
riscv.uart0Body: Writing a byte to 0x60000000 (UART0 FIFO) triggers the onSerialData callback, which streams characters to the Serial Monitor. Reading from the same address pops from the receive FIFO (used by Serial.read()). The UART status register always returns 0 (TX ready).
riscv.fileImageParserDesc: Parses merged flash image, extracts segments

setup.label: // project status
setup.heading: Project Status
setup.lead: A comprehensive overview of all features currently implemented in Velxio.
setup.tsLedSolution: Compile first, then click Run. Check pin assignment in the component property dialog.
setup.tsNewCompSolution: See "After bumping wokwi-elements" above - clone wokwi-elements into third-party/ then run npm run generate:metadata.

esp32.label: // xtensa
esp32.heading: ESP32 Emulation (Xtensa)
esp32.lead: ESP32 and ESP32-S3 boards use an Xtensa LX6 / LX7 architecture. Because no production-quality Xtensa emulator is available as pure JavaScript, Velxio uses a QEMU-based backend for these boards, the lcgamboa fork with libqemu-xtensa, compiled to a native binary and served by the FastAPI backend.
esp32.noteCallout: Note: This section applies to ESP32, ESP32-S3 (Xtensa) and ESP32-C3 (RISC-V) - they all share the same QEMU lcgamboa pipeline, just with different libqemu binaries and machine targets. See RISC-V Emulation (ESP32-C3) for the C3-specific differences.
esp32.how1: Arduino sketch is compiled by arduino-cli to an ESP32 .bin flash image.
esp32.how2: Frontend sends the binary to the backend via WebSocket (Esp32Bridge).
esp32.how3: Backend spawns a QEMU process with the lcgamboa Xtensa plugin, loads the image.
esp32.how4: GPIO and UART events are forwarded over the WebSocket back to the browser.
esp32.how5: Frontend updates component states (LEDs, display, etc.) in real time.
esp32.requirementsBody: QEMU-based emulation requires the Velxio backend to be running. This means it works with the hosted version at velxio.dev and with Docker self-hosting, but not in a pure static frontend deployment.

roadmap.label: // future
roadmap.heading: Roadmap
roadmap.lead: Features that are implemented, in progress, and planned for future releases of Velxio.
roadmap.impMonaco: Monaco Editor with C++ syntax highlighting, autocomplete, and minimap
roadmap.impMultiFile: Multi-file workspace, create, rename, delete, and switch between files
roadmap.impAvr: Real ATmega328p / ATmega2560 emulation at 16 MHz via avr8js
roadmap.impPeripherals: Full GPIO, Timers, USART, ADC, SPI, I2C support
roadmap.impRp2040: Real RP2040 emulation at 133 MHz via rp2040js
roadmap.impWires: Wire creation, orthogonal routing, and segment-based editing
roadmap.impSerial: Serial Monitor with auto baud-rate detection and send support
roadmap.impLibMgr: Library Manager (browse and install Arduino libraries)
roadmap.impProjects: Project save, update, delete with permanent URLs
roadmap.impDocker: Single-container Docker image published to GHCR + Docker Hub
roadmap.ipWires: Functional wire connections, electrical signal routing and validation
roadmap.ipWireErrors: Wire connection error handling, detect short circuits and invalid connections
roadmap.ntUndoRedo: Undo / redo for code edits and canvas changes
roadmap.ntBreadboard: Breadboard, place components with automatic wire routing
roadmap.mtTypeDoc: TypeDoc API documentation, auto-generated from source code
roadmap.mtGhPages: GitHub Pages docs site, automatic deployment on push to main
roadmap.mtSensors: More sensor simulations, HC-SR04, DHT22, IR receiver
roadmap.mtEeprom: EEPROM emulation, persistent read/write across simulation restarts
roadmap.mtScope: Oscilloscope component, plot analog pin voltages over time
roadmap.ltMultiplayer: Multiplayer, share and co-edit simulations in real time
roadmap.ltTutorials: Embedded tutorial system, step-by-step guided projects inside the editor
roadmap.ltSdk: Custom component SDK, define new components with a JSON/TypeScript API
roadmap.ltMobile: Mobile / tablet support, responsive layout for touch devices
roadmap.contributeCallout: Want to contribute? Feature requests, bug reports, and pull requests are welcome at github.com/davidmonterocrespo24/velxio.

emulator.label: // internals
emulator.heading: Emulator Architecture
emulator.lead: Velxio uses real CPU emulation rather than a simplified model. This document describes how each layer of the simulation works.
emulator.avr8Body: The AVR backend uses avr8js, which implements a complete ATmega328p / ATmega2560 processor.
emulator.execLoopBody: Each animation frame executes approximately 267,000 CPU cycles (16 MHz / 60 FPS):
emulator.perUsartDetail: Full transmit and receive, powers the Serial Monitor
emulator.perSpiDetail: Hardware SPI (enables ILI9341, SD card, etc.)
emulator.perI2cDetail: Hardware I2C with virtual device bus
emulator.rp2040Bullet2: UART0 serial output displayed in the Serial Monitor
emulator.rp2040Bullet3: 12-bit ADC on GPIO 26-29 (A0-A3) with 3.3 V reference
emulator.hexBody: Arduino compilation produces Intel HEX format. The parser in hexParser.ts:
emulator.hex2: Extracts the address, record type, and data bytes
emulator.hex4: AVRSimulator converts this to a Uint16Array (16-bit words, little-endian)
emulator.filePinManagerDesc: Maps Arduino pins to UI components

architecture.label: // system design
architecture.heading: Project Architecture
architecture.lead: Velxio is a fully local Arduino emulator using official Wokwi repositories for maximum compatibility. It features real AVR8 CPU emulation, 48+ interactive electronic components, a comprehensive wire system, and a build-time component discovery pipeline.
architecture.routeCompileDesc: Compile sketch files - Intel HEX / UF2
architecture.wireSysLead: Wires are stored as objects with start/end endpoints tied to component pin positions:
architecture.wireBullet2: Segment drag, drag perpendicular to segment orientation
architecture.wireBullet3: Auto-update, wire positions recalculate when components move
architecture.wireBullet4: Grid snapping, 20 px grid for all wire endpoints

intro.label: // overview
intro.heading: Introduction
intro.lead: Velxio is a fully local, open-source Arduino emulator that runs entirely in your browser. Write Arduino C++ code, compile it with a real arduino-cli backend, and simulate it using true AVR8 / RP2040 CPU emulation, with 48+ interactive electronic components, all without installing any software on your machine.
intro.whyNoInstall: No installation required: everything runs in the browser.
intro.whyRealEmulation: Real emulation: not a simplified model, but accurate AVR8 / RP2040 CPU emulation.
intro.whyInteractive: Interactive components: LEDs, buttons, potentiometers, displays, sensors, and more.
intro.whyOpenSource: Open-source: inspect, modify, and self-host it yourself.
intro.liveDemoCallout: Live Demo: velxio.dev , no installation needed, open the editor and start simulating immediately.
